memberdef.name: GPIO_BANK
memberdef.initializer: XGPIOPS_BANK0 /* Bank 0 of the GPIO Device */

briefdescription.para: This function shows the usage of interrupt functionality of the GPIO device.

detaileddescription.para: It is responsible for initializing the GPIO device, setting up interrupts and providing a foreground loop such that interrupts can occur in the background.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XScuGic driver Instance.
is a pointer to the XGpioPs driver Instance.
is the XPAR_<Gpio_Instance>_PS_DEVICE_ID value from xparameters.h.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

briefdescription.para: This function is the user layer callback function for the bank 0 interrupts of the GPIO device.

detaileddescription.para: It checks if all the switches have been pressed to stop the interrupt processing and exit from the example.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the upper layer callback reference.
is the Interrupt status of the GPIO bank.

briefdescription.para: Main function that invokes the GPIO Interrupt example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the GPIO driver (XGpioPs) in an interrupt driven mode of operation.

detaileddescription.para: The example uses the interrupt capability of the GPIO to detect push button events and set the output LEDs based on the input.

simplesect.para: This example assumes that there is a Uart device in the HW design. This example is to provide support only for zcu102 on ZynqMp Platform and only for zc702 on Zynq Platform. For ZynqMP Platform, Input pin is 22(sw19 on zcu102 board) and Output Pin is 23(DS50 on zcu102 board). For Zynq Platform, Input Pins are 12(sw14 on zc702 board), 14(sw13 on zc702 board) and Output Pin is 10(DS23 on zc702 board). SW15 on zc702 board is a combination of sw13 and sw14. To operate either of the input pins, keep SW15 low(both should be 00). This example supports the VCK190 and VMK180 for Versal, but requires a PL shim. See Answer Record AR# 75677 and Figure 61 in AM011 Versal TRM for more details. Driver supports both PS GPIO and PMC GPIO. For accessing PMC GPIOs application you need to set "GPIO.PmcGpio = 1" otherwise it accesses PS GPIO.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sv 01/18/10 First Release 3.3 ms 04/17/17 Added notes about gpio input and output pin description for zcu102 and zc702 boards, configured Interrupt pin to input pin for proper working of interrupt example. 3.7 sne 12/04/19 Reverted versal example support. 3.8 sne 09/17/20 Added description for Versal PS and PMC GPIO pins. 3.9 sne 11/19/20 Added versal PmcGpio example support. 3.12 gm 07/11/23 Added SDT support. 3.15 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues
